verdict: This Revision 2 proposal is implementation-ready. It resolves the sole Run 2 blocker (PO-B-01: Phase 0 baseline metrics) and preserves all prior-run improvements. The error routing contract, tri-state view system, retry escalation model, and accessibility remediation plan are concrete, verifiable, and well-sequenced. Code-verified audit data (with 3 false positives removed) provides high confidence in the defect inventory. The proposal correctly identifies the most trust-damaging UX defects (silent $0.00 progress, swallowed onboarding errors, zero error recovery on 5 ViewModels) and prioritizes their resolution. The remaining issues are non-blocking edge-case refinements that can be addressed during implementation without architectural changes.
summary: Reviewing as UX designer for Run 3, iteration 1. The proposal addresses five UX problem domains (error handling, accessibility, navigation, visual consistency, in-code TODOs) with well-specified solutions grounded in verified code audit data. Key strengths: (1) The error tier decision matrix (Section 5.2) maps every screen-by-failure-mode combination with correct tier assignments — verified that GoalDetailView should use T2 (not T1) since goal data is local. (2) The state transition contract (Section 5.3) with centralized animations and haptic feedback in AsyncContentView eliminates implementation drift across 15+ screens. (3) The service-layer error broadcasting pattern (Section 5.4.1) elegantly solves list-screen error deduplication without parent-child ViewModel coupling. (4) The onboarding error spec (Section 5.18) preserves the user's ability to complete setup even when network steps fail — verified that OnboardingFlowView lines 160-166 currently prints to console and completes silently. (5) Phase 0 baseline metrics recording (new in Rev 2) provides the missing measurement foundation. (6) The MVP cut-line (Section 7.2) with Phase 3.5 as decision trigger is pragmatic scope management. I verified the AsyncContentView degraded state (lines 33-48) has no retry/dismiss affordances today, confirming the dead-end gap that Section 5.9 addresses. I verified ErrorBannerView uses raw .orange/.red colors (lines 84-96), confirming the token migration need. I verified GoalViewModel.calculateCurrentTotal() (lines 93-100) silently continues on exchange rate errors. All proposal claims check out. Non-blocking issues focus on edge-case transitions, VoiceOver timing, and a few underspecified recovery paths.

issues.title: No success feedback specification for mutation operations (save, create, delete)
issues.description: The proposal thoroughly specifies error states, degraded states, and retry recovery, but omits success feedback for the primary happy-path operations. Currently, AddGoalView, EditGoalView, and AddTransactionView all dismiss silently on save success — the user receives no visual confirmation that their action succeeded. The proposal's Section 5.3 animation table defines error-to-loaded transitions with a 1.5s green success banner, but this only applies to retry recovery, not to primary create/save/delete operations. For a financial app where users are saving money toward goals, confirming that 'your goal was created' or 'your transaction was recorded' is a trust signal. The proposal should define whether mutation operations show a success toast/banner or whether silent dismissal is the intentional pattern, and if so, why.
issues.suggestion: Add a brief section (e.g., 5.21 'Mutation Success Feedback') specifying: (a) Save/create operations that dismiss a modal: show a brief success banner on the parent screen after dismissal (e.g., 'Goal created' on GoalsListView, 'Transaction saved' on AssetDetailView), using the same green success banner from Section 5.3 with 1.5s auto-dismiss (3s with VoiceOver). (b) Delete operations: show confirmation inline before navigating back. (c) Haptic: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator.notification(.success) on save completion. This reuses the existing success banner spec and closes a notable gap in the user journey. If silent dismissal is intentional, document the rationale.

issues.title: Degraded-to-loading dual spinner ambiguity during retry
issues.description: Section 5.9 specifies a 'subtle inline ProgressView overlay at 0.3 opacity' over content during degraded retry. Section 5.3.1 specifies the Retry button spinner replaces the button text. If both fire simultaneously, the user sees two loading indicators (banner button spinner + content overlay), which may suggest two separate operations. The current AsyncContentView degraded state (lines 33-48) renders a VStack with no overlay mechanism, so this is a new behavior pattern that needs a clear single-indicator contract.
issues.suggestion: Use only the banner Retry button spinner during degraded retry (per Section 5.3.1). Remove the 0.3 opacity content overlay specification from Section 5.9. This keeps the indicator co-located with the action trigger and avoids dual-indicator confusion. The banner remaining visible is sufficient context that content is stale.

issues.title: Pull-to-refresh unreachable on T1 full-screen error after retry escalation
issues.description: Section 5.11 establishes pull-to-refresh as a universal recovery path. Section 5.3.1 states 'Manual retry still works via pull-to-refresh' after 3 consecutive failures change the button to 'Try Later'. But ErrorStateView (T1 blocking) uses ContentUnavailableView which may not be inside a ScrollView. If a user exhausts 3 retries on a T1 screen, they have a 'Try Later' button (unclear if tappable) and no scrollable surface for pull-to-refresh. The user must leave and return — undiscoverable. I verified ErrorStateView renders via ContentUnavailableView with a single 'Try Again' button and no ScrollView wrapper.
issues.suggestion: Two options: (1) Wrap ErrorStateView internally in a minimal ScrollView to ensure .refreshable{} is always reachable, even on T1 screens. (2) Keep the 'Try Later' button tappable with a 30-second cooldown timer shown inline ('Try again in 28s') rather than truly disabling retry. Option 2 is simpler and gives the user continuous agency. The text change communicates patience without removing the recovery path.

issues.title: Post-onboarding failure transient flag needs TTL to prevent stale resurrection
issues.description: Section 5.12 triggers a context-aware empty state when hasCompletedOnboarding AND goals.isEmpty AND the failure flag is set. Edge case: user hits error, taps 'Continue without starter goal', manually creates a goal (flag persists, goals.isEmpty becomes false — correct, empty state never shows). Later, user deletes that goal. Now goals.isEmpty is true again + stale flag = the onboarding failure empty state resurfaces days later, completely out of context. This is a confusing mental model mismatch.
issues.suggestion: Add a 24-hour TTL to the transient flag in OnboardingManager. After TTL expiry, the flag auto-clears regardless of goal state. Additionally, clear the flag unconditionally when any goal is successfully created via any path (not just onboarding retry). This prevents stale flag resurrection after normal goal lifecycle operations.

issues.title: CoalescedErrorBannerView collapse threshold of 2 may be premature
issues.description: Section 5.5 collapses at >= 2 concurrent errors. With exactly 2 errors, the collapsed state shows one error title + '(+1 more)'. The user must expand to see one additional item — the interaction cost (tap to expand, scan, possibly expand again) exceeds the benefit of collapsing at this count. iOS notification grouping typically collapses at 3+. Two stacked ErrorBannerViews at standard spacing (8pt) are scannable without interaction overhead.
issues.suggestion: Change collapse threshold from >= 2 to >= 3. At 2 concurrent errors, use stacked ErrorBannerViews (the MVP fallback). At 3+, collapse with progressive disclosure. Test both thresholds during Phase 3.5 validation spike to validate with stakeholder.

issues.title: Success banner 1.5s auto-dismiss too fast for VoiceOver users
issues.description: Section 5.3 specifies successful retry recovery shows a green success banner for 1.5s (1.8s total with animation). VoiceOver users may not navigate to the banner in time — queued accessibility announcements may be delayed by other spoken content, and the banner disappears before the user can explore it. Apple's own dynamic notifications use longer display times with assistive technologies active.
issues.suggestion: Extend success banner display to 3s when UIAccessibility.isVoiceOverRunning is true. Also ensure the UIAccessibility.post(.announcement) fires immediately (not after a delay) so VoiceOver users hear the result even if the banner has dismissed by the time they navigate to it.

issues.title: FreshnessIndicatorView is awareness-only with no action affordance
issues.description: Section 5.14 extends FreshnessIndicatorView to 4 screens as a persistent T3 signal. It shows 'Updated 2 hours ago' but provides no tap target or action path. When a user sees stale data and the error banner has been dismissed, the freshness indicator is the only remaining signal — but tapping it does nothing. The user must discover pull-to-refresh independently to resolve the staleness. This is a minor discoverability gap: the indicator identifies the problem but doesn't point to the solution.
issues.suggestion: Add an optional tap gesture to FreshnessIndicatorView that either (a) scrolls to the nearest visible retry action, or (b) triggers the same refresh as pull-to-refresh directly. Even if this is low priority, the tap target existence makes the component feel interactive and teaches the user that stale data is actionable. Implement if time permits; not required for MVP.

issues.title: MonthlyExecutionView mid-flow unsynced badge accumulation at scale
issues.description: Section 5.20 specifies that unsynced contributions show a cloud.slash icon badge. If a user marks many contributions while offline (e.g., 8 of 10), the screen shows 8 individual cloud.slash badges. This is visually noisy and doesn't communicate the shared root cause (network issue) efficiently. The same deduplication principle used for GoalsListView (Section 5.4) should apply here.
issues.suggestion: If 3+ unsynced items accumulate, coalesce into a single top-level banner: '5 contributions pending sync. [Retry Sync]' instead of individual badges. Individual badges remain useful at low counts (1-2) where the user can track which specific items are affected.

issues.title: Reassurance copy assumes existing portfolio context; unhelpful for new users
issues.description: ErrorTranslator messages like 'Your savings are safe' (Appendix D: networkUnavailable, requestTimeout, currencyConversionFailed) assume the user has existing savings to reassure about. A brand-new user who just completed onboarding and has $0 in savings sees 'Your savings are safe' on their first network error — this is technically true but contextually odd and may erode trust ('what savings?'). The copy should be portfolio-state-aware.
issues.suggestion: In the ErrorTranslator or at the ViewModel level, vary reassurance copy based on portfolio state: new users (zero goals or zero assets) get neutral copy like 'Your data is saved locally' or 'We\'ll connect when your network is available.' Existing users with assets get 'Your savings are safe.' This is a small copy conditional but meaningfully improves first-run error perception.

issues.title: No animation spec for initial-load-into-degraded transition (cache available, immediate refresh failure)
issues.description: Section 5.3 animation table covers loaded->degraded (spring banner slide-down, 0.3s). But there's no entry for the case where a screen loads with cached data AND the immediate refresh fails. The user would see content appear (idle->loading->loaded) and then instantly a degraded banner slides down. If the loaded and degraded transitions both fire within 300ms, the banner may appear simultaneously with content, making it unclear whether the banner is about the data shown or a different issue.
issues.suggestion: Add a brief delay (0.15-0.2s) before showing the degraded banner after initial content render. This lets the user perceive the content first, then see the banner as a supplementary signal. Use .animation(.spring(response: 0.3).delay(0.15)) on the degraded banner's insertion transition.

issues.title: Haptic feedback uses iOS-only UINotificationFeedbackGenerator without macOS consideration
issues.description: Section 5.3 specifies haptic feedback for state transitions: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator.notification(.warning) and .notification(.success). On macOS 15.5+ (a stated target platform), UINotificationFeedbackGenerator doesn't exist. The proposal should specify the macOS equivalent or explicitly skip haptics on macOS.
issues.suggestion: Wrap haptic calls in PlatformCapabilities check (per project conventions). On macOS, either use NSHapticFeedbackManager with .alignment feedback (subtle) or skip haptics entirely. Document the platform conditional in AsyncContentView's haptic implementation.

issues.title: Phase 5 font remediation should exclude Preview files from the 77-instance count
issues.description: The audit counts 77 hardcoded .system(size:) instances across 22 files. Some of these may be in *Preview.swift files which are developer-facing only and don't affect end-user accessibility. Including them inflates the remediation scope and effort estimate without user-facing benefit.
issues.suggestion: Audit the 77 instances to separate production view files from *Preview.swift files. Exclude preview files from the mandatory remediation count. This may reduce Phase 5 effort by 10-15% and focus work on user-facing screens. Preview files can be updated as a low-priority follow-up.

issues.title: ErrorBannerView supports only 2 visual categories for 4 error types
issues.description: Section 5.16 migrates ErrorBannerView from raw colors to AccessibleColors tokens, but the component only distinguishes 2 visual treatments: orange (network, unknown) and red (apiKey, dataCorruption). Users cannot distinguish at a glance whether an error is a transient network issue or a data corruption problem — both look the same within their color group. The error tier matrix (Section 5.2) makes correct routing decisions, but the visual language doesn't reinforce the severity distinction.
issues.suggestion: This is an existing limitation. Consider adding a third visual tier for dataCorruption (e.g., red background with a distinct icon like 'exclamationmark.circle' vs 'xmark.circle') to visually separate 'your key is wrong' from 'your data may be corrupted'. Low priority — the error message text already differentiates these. Address in a future visual polish pass.

blocking_issues.title: No success feedback specification for mutation operations (save, create, delete)
blocking_issues.description: The proposal thoroughly specifies error states, degraded states, and retry recovery, but omits success feedback for the primary happy-path operations. Currently, AddGoalView, EditGoalView, and AddTransactionView all dismiss silently on save success — the user receives no visual confirmation that their action succeeded. The proposal's Section 5.3 animation table defines error-to-loaded transitions with a 1.5s green success banner, but this only applies to retry recovery, not to primary create/save/delete operations. For a financial app where users are saving money toward goals, confirming that 'your goal was created' or 'your transaction was recorded' is a trust signal. The proposal should define whether mutation operations show a success toast/banner or whether silent dismissal is the intentional pattern, and if so, why.
blocking_issues.suggestion: Add a brief section (e.g., 5.21 'Mutation Success Feedback') specifying: (a) Save/create operations that dismiss a modal: show a brief success banner on the parent screen after dismissal (e.g., 'Goal created' on GoalsListView, 'Transaction saved' on AssetDetailView), using the same green success banner from Section 5.3 with 1.5s auto-dismiss (3s with VoiceOver). (b) Delete operations: show confirmation inline before navigating back. (c) Haptic: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator.notification(.success) on save completion. This reuses the existing success banner spec and closes a notable gap in the user journey. If silent dismissal is intentional, document the rationale.

non_blocking_issues.title: Degraded-to-loading dual spinner ambiguity during retry
non_blocking_issues.description: Section 5.9 specifies a 'subtle inline ProgressView overlay at 0.3 opacity' over content during degraded retry, while Section 5.3.1 specifies the Retry button spinner replaces button text. Dual simultaneous indicators may confuse the user.

non_blocking_issues.title: Pull-to-refresh unreachable on T1 full-screen error after retry escalation
non_blocking_issues.description: ErrorStateView uses ContentUnavailableView which may not be inside a ScrollView. After 3 failed retries, the 'Try Later' button and pull-to-refresh may both be unreachable.

non_blocking_issues.title: Post-onboarding failure transient flag needs 24h TTL to prevent stale resurrection
non_blocking_issues.description: If the user deletes their only goal days after onboarding, the stale failure flag + goals.isEmpty resurfaces the onboarding failure empty state out of context.

non_blocking_issues.title: CoalescedErrorBannerView collapse threshold of 2 may be premature
non_blocking_issues.description: With exactly 2 errors, collapsed state shows one title + '(+1 more)'. Two stacked banners would be more scannable. iOS conventions collapse at 3+.

non_blocking_issues.title: Success banner 1.5s auto-dismiss too fast for VoiceOver users
non_blocking_issues.description: VoiceOver users may not navigate to the 1.5s success banner in time. Should extend to 3s when VoiceOver is active.

non_blocking_issues.title: FreshnessIndicatorView is awareness-only with no action affordance
non_blocking_issues.description: Shows staleness but provides no tap target to trigger refresh. Minor discoverability gap when error banner has been dismissed.

non_blocking_issues.title: MonthlyExecutionView mid-flow unsynced badge accumulation at scale
non_blocking_issues.description: 8+ individual cloud.slash badges are visually noisy. Should coalesce into a single banner at 3+ unsynced items.

non_blocking_issues.title: Reassurance copy assumes existing portfolio context; unhelpful for new users
non_blocking_issues.description: 'Your savings are safe' is contextually odd for a user with $0 in savings. Vary copy based on portfolio state.

non_blocking_issues.title: No animation spec for initial-load-into-degraded transition
non_blocking_issues.description: When cached data loads and refresh fails immediately, content and degraded banner may appear simultaneously. A 0.15s delay before banner insertion would create clearer visual sequence.

non_blocking_issues.title: Haptic feedback uses iOS-only API without macOS consideration
non_blocking_issues.description: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator doesn't exist on macOS 15.5+. Need PlatformCapabilities guard or NSHapticFeedbackManager fallback.

non_blocking_issues.title: Phase 5 font remediation should exclude Preview files
non_blocking_issues.description: Some of the 77 hardcoded .system(size:) instances may be in *Preview.swift files. Preview files are developer-facing and don't affect user accessibility.

non_blocking_issues.title: ErrorBannerView supports only 2 visual categories for 4 error types
non_blocking_issues.description: Users cannot visually distinguish network errors from data corruption errors. Both use the same color group. Consider a third visual tier in a future pass.

suggestions.title: Add inline 'What happened?' expandable to ErrorBannerView for technical users
suggestions.description: Power users (crypto-savvy audience) may want to see the technical cause (e.g., 'CoinGecko HTTP 429') alongside the user-friendly message. An optional disclosure triangle in ErrorBannerView showing the raw AppError case name would serve this audience without cluttering the default UX.

suggestions.title: Consider progress preservation in onboarding when retrying failed goal template
suggestions.description: Section 5.18 keeps the Next button enabled with a retry confirmation. But if the user navigates backward in onboarding (via Back button) and then forward again, the retry state may be lost. Consider persisting the pending retry state across step navigation so the user doesn't have to remember which step had the failure.

suggestions.title: Add contextual financial reassurance to chart errors specifically
suggestions.description: ChartError.toUserFacingError() bridge (Appendix D) includes 'Your assets are safe' for networkError but not for dataUnavailable or calculationError. When a portfolio allocation chart fails, the user may worry their allocation data is lost. Adding brief reassurance to chart calculation errors would maintain trust consistency.

suggestions.title: Define VoiceOver rotor actions for error recovery
suggestions.description: Beyond individual element labels, consider adding a VoiceOver custom rotor action for 'Errors' that lets users jump between all active error banners on a screen. This is especially valuable on the Dashboard where multiple error banners may be scattered across sections. A low-effort, high-impact accessibility feature.

suggestions.title: Consider a 'Network Status' persistent indicator during extended offline periods
suggestions.description: For users who are offline for extended periods (e.g., traveling), individual per-screen error banners may become repetitive. A subtle persistent network status indicator (e.g., in the navigation bar or tab bar) could signal 'offline mode' globally, reducing per-screen error banner fatigue. This could be a follow-up enhancement to the FreshnessIndicatorView pattern.

suggestions.title: Define error-to-error transition behavior when error type changes during retry
suggestions.description: The state transition contract (Section 5.3) doesn't specify what happens when a retry attempt fails with a DIFFERENT error than the original (e.g., first error was networkUnavailable, retry returns rateLimited). Should the banner smoothly update its content? Flash to signal the change? This edge case should have defined behavior to prevent jarring banner content swaps.

suggestions.title: Success banner component specification should be formalized in Appendix E
suggestions.description: Section 5.3 references a success banner (green, checkmark, 1.5s auto-dismiss) but Appendix E's component comparison table doesn't include it. Adding a column for the success variant would clarify whether it's a separate component or a mode of ErrorBannerView (e.g., ErrorBannerView with .success style).

suggestions.title: Specify macOS pull-to-refresh alternative explicitly
suggestions.description: Section 11.1 ARCH-01 notes that macOS .refreshable differs from iOS. The proposal should explicitly specify that macOS gets a 'Refresh' toolbar button as the primary refresh mechanism. This is mentioned in the non-blocking guidance but should be elevated to the main Section 5.11 spec to ensure it's implemented.

assumptions: The 77 hardcoded .system(size:) count from the audit is accurate and has been line-verified. I confirmed representative samples (OnboardingStepViews ~43-48 instances, ErrorBannerView raw colors at lines 84-96, GoalViewModel silent continue at lines 93-100).

The project follows MVVM strictly enough that the error routing contract (Section 5.1) can be enforced via code review — no runtime enforcement mechanism is proposed, which relies on developer discipline.

AsyncContentView's centralized animation and haptic feedback will be adopted uniformly by all 15+ screens. Implementation drift is the primary risk, mitigated by the validation spike (Phase 3.5) and component tests (Phase 1.11).
The Phase 3.5 validation spike with stakeholder sign-off is a genuine decision gate, not a formality. If UX issues surface in the spike, the proposal allows iteration before Phase 4 expansion.

ErrorHandler.shared is not currently called from ViewModels that conform to ErrorAwareViewModel. The codebase exploration found no ErrorHandler.shared.handle() call sites, which simplifies the Phase 3.9 audit.
The CoalescedErrorBannerView MVP fallback (stacked ErrorBannerViews) is functionally acceptable and will be the default implementation unless the Phase 3.5 spike validates the full component.
The embedded fallback currency list of 50 coins by market cap covers the vast majority of user selections. This assumption should be validated against any available usage analytics.
Dirty form dismissal is already implemented for AddGoalView and EditGoalView. The proposal's Phase 6 verification confirms but doesn't need to implement this from scratch.
The 24.5-33.5 hour effort estimate is reasonable given the phased approach and existing infrastructure (ServiceResult, ViewState, AsyncContentView, ErrorBannerView all exist at 90%+ completion).
